tool_output.output:
# Globalstar FY2025 Financial Summary

Based on the provided 10-K excerpts, here are the key financial metrics for the year ended December 31, 2025:

## Income Statement Metrics

| Metric | FY2025 |
|---|---|
| **Total Revenue** | **$273.0 million** ($272,986 thousand) — up 9% from $250.3 million in 2024 |
| — Service revenue | $257.3 million (94% of total) |
| — Subscriber equipment sales | $15.7 million (6% of total) |
| **Operating Income** | **$7.4 million** ($7,430 thousand) — a turnaround from a loss of $(0.9) million in 2024 |
| **Net Loss** | **$(8.7) million** ($(8,651) thousand) — improved from $(63.2) million in 2024 |

## Adjusted EBITDA

**Adjusted EBITDA is not explicitly disclosed** in the provided chunks. The excerpts include the Consolidated Statements of Operations and cash flow data but do not contain an Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table. (Companies typically present this as a non-GAAP measure in their MD&A or earnings releases; it does not appear in the segments provided.)

## Balance Sheet — As of December 31, 2025

| Metric | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Total Debt (principal)** | **$410.0 million** (weighted average stated rate up to 9%) |
| **Cash and Cash Equivalents** | **$447.5 million** ($447,471 thousand) |

## Share Count (Cover Page)

The **cover page share count is not included** in the provided chunks. The excerpts begin with the service revenue table and financial statement sections but do not reproduce the Form 10-K cover page, which would typically state shares outstanding as of a recent date (e.g., late February 2026). The EPS note references 1.8 million anti-dilutive shares excluded for 2025 and 3.3 million customer warrant shares, but the basic shares outstanding figure from the cover page is not available in the text provided.

---

### Key Contextual Notes
- **Revenue growth** was driven primarily by higher wholesale capacity services revenue ($172.7M vs. $145.3M), reflecting the Updated Services Agreements with the Customer (Apple), which accounted for **63% of total revenue** in 2025.
- **Deferred revenue, net** stood at **$869.0 million** at year-end, the majority of which will be earned over a period exceeding five years.
- The **Globalstar SPE** (Special Purpose Entity) held total assets of $824.6 million as of December 31, 2025, including $653.7 million in net property and equipment for the Extended MSS Network.

tool_output.output:
# Vertiv Holdings FY2025 Financial Summary

## Income Statement Metrics (Year Ended December 31, 2025)

| Metric | Amount ($ millions) |
|---|---|
| **Net sales (Revenue)** | **$10,229.9** |
| — Products | $8,390.6 |
| — Services | $1,839.3 |
| **Operating profit** | **$1,829.7** |
| **Net income** | **$1,332.8** |
| **Adjusted EBITDA** | *Not disclosed in the provided chunks* |

> **Note on Adjusted EBITDA:** The provided excerpts include the GAAP Consolidated Statements of Earnings and MD&A liquidity/debt discussion, but do not contain the non-GAAP reconciliation table that would report Adjusted EBITDA. Operating profit of $1,829.7M is the closest GAAP figure available; adding back the disclosed amortization of intangibles ($200.4M) and restructuring costs ($54.5M) would yield a partial proxy, but depreciation expense (embedded in cost of sales) and other adjustments are not separately quantified in these chunks.

## Share Count (Cover Page)

| Date | Shares Outstanding |
|---|---|
| **As of February 9, 2026** | **382,598,464** Class A common shares |

(Weighted-average basic shares for FY2025: 381,712,181; diluted: 390,652,824)

## Balance Sheet Position as of December 31, 2025

### Total Debt

| Instrument | Principal ($ millions) |
|---|---|
| Senior Secured Notes due 2028 | $850.0 |
| Term Loan due 2032 | $2,076.1 |
| ABL Revolving Credit Facility due 2029 | $0.0 (nothing drawn) |
| **Total debt** | **$2,926.1** |

### Cash Position

| Item | Amount ($ millions) |
|---|---|
| Cash and cash equivalents | $1,728.4 |
| Short-term investments | $99.5 |
| **Total cash & short-term investments** | **$1,827.9** |

### Net Debt

$2,926.1 (total debt) − $1,827.9 (cash + short-term investments) = **~$1,098.2 million**

Additionally, Vertiv had **$784.0 million** of undrawn availability under the ABL Revolving Credit Facility (net of $16.0M in letters of credit), providing further liquidity headroom.
